steps.title: Use isosceles geometry to set up a right triangle
steps.content: Triangle RST is isosceles with RS = ST and base RT = 48. When we drop a perpendicular from apex S to the base RT, it bisects the base at point M. Therefore, RM = 24.

steps.title: Apply the tangent ratio to find the height
steps.content:
In right triangle RSM, the angle at R has a tangent ratio of opposite over adjacent:

$$\tan R = \frac{SM}{RM}$$

Substituting $\tan R = \frac{7}{24}$ and $RM = 24$:

$$\frac{SM}{24} = \frac{7}{24}$$

Therefore, SM = 7 units.

steps.title: Calculate the area using base and height
steps.content:
The area of the triangle is:

$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 48 \times 7 = 168$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
